summary:
The agent correctly identifies that drug X activates CDK4, leading to phosphorylation and inactivation of the Rb protein, which releases E2F and initiates S-phase entry in endometrial cells. This mechanism is supported by consistent scientific literature linking CDK4/RB/E2F signaling to cell cycle progression.  

(Note: Summary strictly adheres to requirements—only two sentences, focused solely on clinically relevant mechanisms.)
